channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Contas do governo têm déficit de R$ 30 bilhões em fevereiro
item.description: The Brazilian government recorded a primary deficit of R$ 30 billion in February, an improvement compared to the R$ 32.8 billion primary surplus in the same month of 2024 (inflation-adjusted). The 2026 fiscal target is a primary surplus of 0.25% of GDP (~R$ 34.3 billion), with a tolerance band of ±0.25 percentage points and legal allowances to exclude up to R$ 63.5 billion in spending, such as court-ordered payments (precatórios). With these adjustments, the official forecast projects a primary deficit of nearly R$ 60 billion in 2026, implying negative fiscal balances throughout Lula’s third term.

item.title: Ibaneis Rocha deixa governo do DF sem solucionar crise do BRB criada durante sua gestão
item.description: Ibaneis Rocha (MDB) left the Federal District government without resolving the crisis at the Bank of Brasília (BRB), which occurred during his administration and is now inherited by his former vice governor, Celina Leão (PP), who assumes office as governor. The crisis stems from fraudulent operations with Banco Master, leading to losses and risk of temporary administration or privatization of the public bank. Ibaneis, now a Senate candidate, avoided direct treasury funding to avoid political backlash, worsening his political wear in the electoral race.

item.title: STF decide em 8 de abril se eleição para mandato-tampão no Rio será direta ou indireta
item.description: The Supreme Federal Court (STF) has set April 8 to rule on whether the interim governor election in Rio de Janeiro will be indirect (chosen by the State Legislative Assembly) or direct (by popular vote). Minister Cristiano Zanin previously suspended the indirect election and ordered the president of the Rio de Janeiro Court of Justice to remain in office until the STF decides. Following the resignation of Cláudio Castro to run for re-election and his subsequent ineligibility, and with the vice-governor and the ALERJ president also barred or removed, the state's succession process is at a standstill.

item.title: Eduardo Cunha é o entrevistado desta segunda do Frente a Frente, programa de Folha e UOL
item.description: Former Chamber of Deputies president Eduardo Cunha is the interviewee on this Monday's (30) Frente a Frente program, a weekly interview series by Folha and UOL focused on the 2026 elections. The discussion covers his views and positioning in the current political landscape.

item.title: STF marca para dia 8 sessão de julgamento sobre nova eleição no RJ
item.description: Supreme Federal Court (STF) President Minister Edson Fachin has scheduled April 8 for the ruling on petitions calling for new gubernatorial elections in Rio de Janeiro. The move follows legal challenges questioning the validity of the 2022 state election, with potential consequences for the state's political stability.

item.title: Caiado é escolhido pelo PSD para disputar a Presidência nas eleições 2026
item.description: The governor of Goiás, Ronaldo Caiado, has been chosen by the PSD as the party's presidential candidate for the 2026 elections, following the withdrawal of Ratinho Júnior. The announcement will be made this Monday at the PSD headquarters in São Paulo, solidifying Caiado as the party's leading option, despite internal opposition favoring Eduardo Leite as a more centrist alternative.

item.title: Pesquisa Atlas: Tarcísio derrota qualquer oponente em SP e tem chances de vencer no 1º turno
item.description: The AtlasIntel poll shows that Governor Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicanos) leads all electoral scenarios for re-election in São Paulo, with a chance to win in the first round against candidates like Fernando Haddad (PT), Geraldo Alckmin (PSB), Márcio França (PSB) and Simone Tebet (PSB). In second-round scenarios, Tarcísio also wins by expressive margins, reinforcing his favoritism in the country's largest electoral college.

item.title: A estratégia de Lula para alimentar rejeição a Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva is implementing a strategy to increase rejection of Senator Flávio Bolsonaro by associating his name with that of former President Jair Bolsonaro, aiming to link the son to the father’s governmental mistakes, particularly those that led to Bolsonaro’s 2022 election defeat. The move seeks to preempt criticism of the rival, as Flávio’s rapid rise in polls has concerned Lula’s team, which observes a technical tie between the two in potential second-round scenarios.

item.title: PSD anuncia hoje Ronaldo Caiado como nome para a Presidência
item.description: The governor of Goiás, Ronaldo Caiado, will be announced today as the PSD’s pre-candidate for the Presidency of the Republic. The decision ends speculation about a possible third way in the 2026 elections, positioning Caiado as an alternative to Lula (PT) and Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ). The formal announcement will be made in a press conference later today.

item.title: STF vai julgar em 8 de abril modelo da eleição para mandato-tampão no Rio de Janeiro
item.description: The STF has scheduled April 8 to rule on whether the interim governor election in Rio de Janeiro will be indirect (via the state legislature) or direct (by popular vote). Minister Cristiano Zanin has already suspended the indirect election pending the decision. The debate centers on concerns about political maneuvering, risks of interference, and technical challenges of holding two popular elections in a short timeframe.

item.title: PSD escolhe Ronaldo Caiado como candidato a presidente
item.description: The PSD has chosen Goiás governor Ronaldo Caiado as its presidential candidate for the 2026 elections, following the withdrawal of Ratinho Junior. The official announcement will be made this Monday (March 30, 2026) at 4 p.m. at the party’s headquarters in São Paulo. Rio Grande do Sul governor Eduardo Leite was passed over and seen only as an internal alternative, with a potential Senate bid if not selected.

item.title: Paraná Pesquisas também aponta Flávio à frente de Lula
item.description: A Paraná Pesquisas poll released on Monday (30) shows Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) leading former President Lula (PT) in a hypothetical presidential runoff, with 45.2% voting intentions versus 44.1% for Lula. Blank and null votes total 6.2%. The results indicate a technical tie between the two candidates.

item.title: Os novos números do instituto Paraná Pesquisas para a eleição presidencial
item.description: The Paraná Pesquisas poll released on March 30, 2026, shows Lula (PT) and Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) technically tied in the first round with 41.3% and 37.8%, respectively, within the 2.2-point margin of error. In a potential runoff, Bolsonaro leads narrowly with 45.2% to Lula’s 44.1%. Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) ranks third at 3.6% after Ratinho Junior withdrew from the race.

item.title: Caiado será o candidato do PSD à Presidência contra Lula
item.description: Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado is being considered as a potential presidential candidate for the PSD party in the 2026 elections, positioning himself as an opponent to President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva. The information emerges amid speculation about party realignments and opposition strategies for the upcoming election.

item.title: BTG/Nexus: Lula e Flávio Bolsonaro empatam em 46% em eventual 2º turno da corrida presidencial
item.description: A Nexus Pesquisa e Inteligência, in partnership with BTG Pactual, found that Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT) and Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) are tied at 46% in voter intentions for a potential 2026 presidential runoff. In the first round, the two are also technically tied, indicating a highly polarized political landscape in Brazil.

item.title: Flávio encosta em Lula no primeiro turno e supera o petista no segundo
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro is closing in on Lula in the first round and technically surpasses him in the second, according to Paraná Pesquisas. In the first round, Lula has 41.3% and Flávio 37.8%, within the 2.2-point margin of error, indicating a technical tie. In the second-round simulation, Flávio leads with 45.2% against Lula’s 44.1%, also within the margin of error. A majority of respondents (53.3%) believe Lula does not deserve re-election.

item.title: Lula e Flávio empatam em disputa pelo Planalto no 1º e no 2º turnos
item.description: Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro are technically tied in both the first and second rounds of the 2026 Brazilian presidential election, according to Paraná Pesquisas. Lula has 41.3% and Flávio 37.8% in the first round (margin of error: 2.2 points), while in the second round Flávio leads with 45.2% against Lula’s 44.1%, also within the margin of error. The unfavorable swing for Lula increased from 0.6 to 1.1 percentage points in one month.

item.title: Paraná Pesquisas: 53,3% dizem que Lula não merece ser reeleito
item.description: According to a Paraná Pesquisas survey conducted from March 25 to 28, 2026, 53.3% of Brazilian voters believe President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT) does not deserve a fourth term, while 43.7% support his re-election. The poll, with a margin of error of 2.2 percentage points and 95% confidence level, shows the South as the most opposed region (66.1%), while the Northeast is the only region where a majority supports Lula’s re-election (54.8%).

item.title: Lula inaugura obras tocadas pelo Ministério da Educação nesta segunda
item.description: President Lula inaugurated 107 educational works across Brazil on Monday, including daycare centers, full-time schools, federal institutes, universities, and university hospitals, funded by the New PAC and the Ministry of Education. He also celebrated that 99,000 public schools now have quality internet access through the National Strategy for Connected Schools, led by the MEC and the Ministry of Communications. Ministers Camilo Santana (Education) and Frederico Siqueira (Communications) attended the event.

item.title: Pacote de bondades de Lula soma R$ 403,2 bi em ano eleitoral
item.description: President Lula has prepared a social spending package of R$ 403.2 billion for 2026, the year he seeks re-election, including expanded income tax exemption, Bolsa Família, Gás do Povo, and housing and business credit programs. Despite extensive promotion, the measures have not boosted his popularity, with polls showing 61% disapproval and 51% rating his performance as bad or terrible.

item.title: Agenda da semana: reunião ministerial e escolha do candidato do PSD
item.description: This week, President Lula announces the inauguration of 107 education projects and holds the final ministerial meeting with the current Esplanada layout. The PSD selects between Ronaldo Caiado and Eduardo Leite as its pre-candidate for the presidency. Additional highlights include the release of the budget decree, employment data release, inauguration of an ITA campus in Fortaleza, ministerial resignations, and the close of the party-switching window.

item.title: PSD anuncia nesta 2ª feira seu candidato à Presidência
item.description: The PSD will announce its presidential candidate this Monday (March 30) between governors Eduardo Leite (RS) and Ronaldo Caiado (GO), following Ratinho Junior’s (PR) withdrawal. According to former governor Jorge Bornhausen, the party’s selection council has already chosen Caiado as its representative, a decision made on March 23. Leite is seen as an alternative and may run for Senate if not selected.

item.title: Sônia Guajajara deixa ministério para disputar eleições
item.description: Sônia Guajajara (Psol) left the Ministry of Indigenous Peoples to run for re-election as a federal deputy from São Paulo. After three years leading the ministry, she will hand over the position to executive secretary Eloy Terena in a ceremony on Tuesday (March 31, 2026) in Brasília. Under her leadership, the ministry homologated 20 indigenous territories and issued 21 declaratory ordinances, highlighted by the largest indigenous participation at COP30 in Belém in 2025.

item.title: Lula até podia ter outros planos, mas não obrigará Alckmin a sair da vice
item.description: Lula may have considered other options for his 2026 ticket, but he will not force Geraldo Alckmin to step down as vice president if Alckmin wishes to stay. Alckmin has the freedom to decide his political future, and Lula will respect his choice. Therefore, the current vice president could remain on the ticket if he chooses to do so.

item.title: Aliados de Flávio veem 'tempestade perfeita' com STF e Lula enfraquecidos
item.description: Allies of Flávio Bolsonaro predict a 'perfect storm' in the 2026 election, citing declining public trust in the Supreme Court due to scandals like the Master Bank case and voter dissatisfaction with Lula's economic management. This combination could bolster Bolsonaro's electoral prospects.

item.title: RJ: eleição indireta com voto secreto para governo favorece candidato do PT
item.description: The indirect election for governor of Rio de Janeiro, with secret ballot, favors André Ceciliano (PT), according to political analysis, if the format supported by the majority of STF ministers is confirmed. The change in the governor selection method could directly impact the 2026 electoral race in the state.

item.title: Eleições 2026: tarifa zero no transporte público pode ser 'Bolsa Família sobre rodas' para Lula?
item.description: Lula appears to have shifted his stance on free public transportation, after criticizing the idea in 2006. His government has now commissioned a study on the national viability of zero-fare transit, which could be included in his 2026 re-election platform as a 'Unified Mobility System,' funded by public budget. The proposal emerges amid declining poll numbers for Lula and rising support for Flávio Bolsonaro, with at least 145 municipalities and nine capitals already implementing some form of free transit.

item.title: Atlas/Estadão: Tarcísio tem 49,1% e Haddad tem 42,6% na disputa em São Paulo
item.description: The Atlas/Estadão poll shows Tarcísio de Freitas with 49.1% of voting intentions against 42.6% for Fernando Haddad in the São Paulo gubernatorial race, with Kim Kataguiri at 5% and Paulo Serra at 1.2%. The margin of error is 2 percentage points at a 95% confidence level. In an alternative scenario, Simone Tebet replaces Haddad with 41.8%, while Tarcísio holds at 48.8%.

item.title: Atlas/Estadão: Haddad só bate Tarcísio entre jovens e idosos, mais ricos e mais escolarizados em SP
item.description: Tarcísio de Freitas leads the São Paulo gubernatorial re-election race with 49.1% in the first round, compared to Fernando Haddad’s 42.6%. His advantage stems from male voters and middle-aged groups (25-59), while Haddad leads among youth (16-24), seniors (60+), higher-income, and more educated voters. Kim Kataguiri shows strong support among young men, reaching 23.7% in the 16-24 age bracket.

item.title: Atlas/Estadão: Tarcísio venceria aliados de Lula no 2.º turno e teria 10 pontos sobre Haddad em SP
item.description: The AtlasIntel/Estadão poll shows that Governor Tarcísio de Freitas would defeat all Lula allies in a potential 2026 second-round runoff in São Paulo. Against former Finance Minister Fernando Haddad, Tarcísio leads with 53.5% to 43.2%, a 10.3-point advantage. Similar leads are observed against Geraldo Alckmin (8.6 points) and Simone Tebet (8.5 points), with a 2-point margin of error and 95% confidence level.

item.title: Fim do governo Ibaneis: relembre fatos marcantes dos últimos 7 anos
item.description: Ibaneis Rocha, who served as governor of the Federal District for seven years and three months, left office to run for the Senate in the 2026 elections. His tenure was marked by managing the Covid-19 pandemic, including lockdowns and restrictions, and a 65-day removal from office in 2023 over alleged negligence, followed by reinstatement after a Supreme Court order. He was the first native of Brasília to hold the position, beginning his political career as a lawyer and leader of the OAB-DF.

item.title: CGU emite 35 mil alertas de indícios de irregularidades na gestão Lula
item.description: The CGU issued 34,733 alerts indicating potential irregularities in federal public procurement processes between 2023 and February 2026, with 729 leading to audit openings and 1,523 formal communications to managers. Although AI tools generated the alerts, the agency lacks a structured database to automatically link each alert to specific outcomes such as edital cancellations or referrals to the PF or MPF, requiring manual analysis for such correlations.

item.title: TRE faz retotalização de votos para permitir nova eleição na Alerj
item.description: The TRE-RJ will conduct a vote retotalization for the 2022 elections to annul the votes of state deputy Rodrigo Bacellar, whose diploma was annulled by the TSE. This measure enables a new election for the presidency of Alerj, following the annulment of 97,822 votes and Bacellar's ineligibility due to misuse of Ceperj funds.

item.title: Juíza assessora do governador interno desiste de ser candidata no RJ
item.description: Renata Gil, a judge and advisor to Rio de Janeiro's interim governor, Justice Ricardo Couto, has withdrawn her candidacy for federal deputy in 2026. She cited political instability in the state and the need for a more conservative judicial stance due to her role. Gil, former president of the Brazilian Magistrates Association (AMB) and partner of STF Minister Dias Toffoli, had accepted an invitation from PSD national president Gilberto Kassab to run.

item.title: Ronaldo Caiado anunciará candidatura ao Planalto nesta segunda-feira
item.description: Governor of Goiás, Ronaldo Caiado, will announce his presidential candidacy this Monday (30) at a press conference at the PSD headquarters in São Paulo. Affiliated with the party since March 14, Caiado was selected after Ratinho Júnior withdrew, competing for the nomination with Eduardo Leite, also from PSD. While the party sees room in the Lula-Bolsonaro polarization, polls indicate extremely low chances of victory.

item.title: Depois acusar deputado por estupro, Soraya diz não ter de provar nada
item.description: Senator Soraya Thronicke (Podemos-MS) stated she has no duty to prove anything after filing a complaint against federal deputy Alfredo Gaspar (PL-AL) for rape of a minor, arguing that paternity investigations in Brazil begin without proof and that refusal to take a DNA test can lead to a presumption of paternity. Along with Deputy Lindbergh Farias (PT-RJ), she submitted a report to the Federal Police alleging the rape of a 13-year-old girl, forced pregnancy, and an attempt to cover up the case through a R$ 470,000 payment, though no concrete evidence was presented at the time of the complaint.

item.title: O Brasil não é um país de descontrolados
item.description: President Lula advocated for a national campaign to teach the population how to manage household budgets, emphasizing financial education as an essential tool to combat debt and promote economic autonomy for families. He stated that Brazil is not a country of reckless people, but of citizens who deserve guidance to make more conscious financial decisions.

item.title: Prêmio da Globo tem piadas sobre Vorcaro e powerpoint errado
item.description: During Globo's Melhores do Ano 2026 award ceremony, comedian Paulo Vieira joked about Daniel Vorcaro, owner of Banco Master, including a comment about not knowing how to make PowerPoint, referencing a controversial diagram aired by GloboNews' Estúdio I about Vorcaro's political connections, which the network later admitted contained flaws. Vieira also mocked intercepted communications between Vorcaro and his ex-girlfriend Martha Graeff, leaked by the Federal Police, quipping that only in Brazil would a 'mobster' say 'momolada'.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro se mostra ‘paz e amor’ mais na forma do que no conteúdo
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro, the PL’s presumptive 2026 presidential candidate, delivered a CPAC speech that attempted to project moderation in tone but retained the radical content of Bolsonarismo. His restless body language and reliance on the teleprompter signaled nervousness and insecurity, undermining his effort to appeal to independents. Despite a less aggressive style, he repeated false claims about the 2022 election and portrayed his father as a political prisoner, revealing that his moderation is superficial and ideological continuity remains intact.

item.title: Eduardo diz gravar vídeo para Bolsonaro, impedido de usar celular
item.description: Eduardo Bolsonaro recorded a video during his participation at CPAC in the United States on Saturday, the 28th, stating that the recording would be shown to his father, former president Jair Bolsonaro. Before Senator Flávio Bolsonaro's speech, Eduardo appeared on stage holding his cell phone and explained the reason for the recording.

item.title: O dilema do PSD: focar no eleitor anti-Lula ou ampliar para além da polarização?
item.description: The PSD faces a strategic dilemma for 2026: compete in the anti-Lula space with Ronaldo Caiado, betting on rejection of the current government, or build a broader alternative with Eduardo Leite, capable of attracting voters tired of the Lula-Bolsonaro polarization. Caiado represents a democratic right with limited appeal beyond conservatives, while Leite offers an equidistant profile, dialoguing with those who reject both poles. The choice defines whether the party seeks immediate competitiveness or long-term repositioning in the political system.

item.title: Paulo Vieira joga fora roteiro ‘certinho’ da Globo e expõe acidez no ‘Domingão’
item.description: During 'Domingão com Huck', Paulo Vieira delivered politically incorrect jokes at the Melhores do Ano awards ceremony, teasing Luciano Huck's political aspirations and making a sarcastic reference to the Daniel Vorcaro case and an alleged PowerPoint linking Lula to the PT. He clarified he did not actually create such a presentation, maintaining a lighthearted and subtly critical tone toward Globo.

item.title: Membros do Psol reagem ao discurso de Flávio em evento conservador
item.description: Members of the PSOL party criticized Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) for his speech at CPAC in Dallas, where he urged the U.S. government to pressure Brazil’s elections. Deputy Erika Hilton called the Bolsonaro family 'parasitic' and accused Flávio of promoting an 'ideological and digital invasion,' while Deputy Tarcísio Motta likened his rhetoric to colonial exploitation. Minister Guilherme Boulos called the incident one of the gravest moments of the 2026 elections, claiming Flávio is publicly offering Brazil’s rare earths and critical minerals to the U.S. if elected.

item.title: Depois de catorze anos, linha Ouro do metrô de São Paulo será inagurada
item.description: São Paulo’s Ouro Line, a 17 km monorail linking Congonhas Airport to Morumbi station, will open after 14 years of construction, initially operating free of charge with reduced hours. Launched in 2012 under Geraldo Alckmin, the project faced delays due to suspensions and government changes, and has recently been criticized by Governor Tarcísio de Freitas targeting Alckmin, now Vice President and a potential political rival.

item.title: Sucessão solta fumaça, não sai do lugar e derrama óleo no chão
item.description: Lula responded to Flávio Bolsonaro's comparison, in which he was called an 'old Opala', saying he was not offended because Bolsonaro's Opala is his father, who is in the junkyard. The remark was made during an event about the 2026 succession, in a tone of irony and ease. Only a portion of the statement has been released so far.

item.title: Passado dos Bolsonaros é de 700 mil covas na pandemia, diz PT
item.description: PT president Edinho Silva accused the Bolsonaro family of causing 700,000 deaths during the pandemic due to scientific denialism, while launching the Senate pre-candidacy of Marília Campos in Minas Gerais as a key strategy for Lula’s 2026 re-election. He rejected the opposition’s portrayal of Flávio Bolsonaro as a political 'empty body,' asserting the family’s legacy is defined by dismantled public health policies and the opening of 700,000 graves in Brazil.

item.title: Fernando Morais lança biografia 'Lula, Volume 2', com trajetória das Diretas Já à eleição de 2002
item.description: Journalist Fernando Morais launches 'Lula, Volume 2', the second part of a biographical trilogy on President Lula, covering his journey from the Diretas Já movement to the 2002 election. The book signing will take place at Livraria da Travessa in Iguatemi Mall, São Paulo, on March 30, 2026.

item.title: Fala de Flávio Bolsonaro nos EUA sobre terras raras revolta a esquerda
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) sparked controversy after stating at a U.S. conservative event (CPAC) that Brazil could reduce U.S. dependence on China for rare earths and critical minerals, while urging international monitoring of Brazilian elections. His remarks were condemned by Lula administration allies as a betrayal of national sovereignty, accusing him of offering Brazil's natural wealth to foreign powers in exchange for political support.

item.title: O que acontece agora após o fim da CPMI do INSS sem relatório aprovado
item.description: The INSS CPI concluded without approving its final report, which recommended indicting 216 people, including President Lula’s son. Despite the rejection, the rapporteur said he will forward the document to the PF, TCU, MP, and Federal Revenue. In parallel, the government base produced an alternative report calling for the indictment of 130 people, including Jair and Flávio Bolsonaro, which will also be sent to authorities.

item.title: Lula é tão desaprovado quanto Bolsonaro a 6 meses da eleição
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva shows disapproval ratings similar to those of Jair Bolsonaro at the same point in their mandates (3 years and 3 months), with 51% negative and 26% positive evaluations, according to PoderData. Both are the only incumbents since redemocratization to run for re-election amid high rejection, with Bolsonaro being the sole one to lose. Lula now holds a record 61% disapproval in binary response polling, signaling significant public wear despite not facing a pandemic like his predecessor.
